summary.intent: Implement a server-side terminal disable feature for the Moltis web UI (GitHub issue #681), with both a config flag and an env var hard override to prevent re-enabling via the web UI config editor.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented and committed: added `terminal_enabled` to `ServerConfig` with `MOLTIS_TERMINAL_DISABLED` env var override, guarded all 3 terminal API routes with 403 responses, injected `terminal_enabled` into gon data, and updated the frontend to show a disabled notice instead of connecting.

repo: WebConfig lives under `[tools.web]`, not a top-level `[web]` section â€” server-level settings belong in `ServerConfig` under `[server]`.
The project uses a `build_schema_map` function in `validate.rs` that must be updated when new config fields are added.
Gon data is built in `crates/web/src/templates.rs` and injected into the frontend; feature flags like `voice_enabled` follow this pattern.
The project's lint config forbids unsafe code entirely (`#![deny(unsafe_code)]`), making `std::env::set_var`/`remove_var` unusable in tests.
Pre-existing CUDA build/test failures exist on macOS â€” clippy and tests must exclude CUDA-dependent crates to get clean results.
The project uses a 'beads' task tracking system with CLI commands for creating and claiming issues before implementation.

code.finding: ServerConfig is the correct struct for server-level feature flags; added `terminal_enabled: bool` with default true and a `is_terminal_enabled()` helper that checks env var `MOLTIS_TERMINAL_DISABLED` as a hard override.
Three terminal routes need guarding: GET /api/terminal/windows, POST /api/terminal/windows, and the WebSocket upgrade handler. Some handlers lacked `State(state)` and needed it added.
Terminal page is rendered as a `PageSection` via pageSections routing; intercept before render when section is 'terminal' and gon.terminal_enabled is false to show a disabled notice.

workflow: Clippy lint `collapsible_if` caught a nested if that should use `&&` â€” formatter doesn't catch this, needs a clippy pass.
Rust 2024 edition (or recent nightly) treats `std::env::set_var`/`remove_var` as unsafe; env var behavior in tests must be verified indirectly or skipped.
Running `cargo fmt` before `cargo clippy` avoids spurious formatting warnings in clippy output.

friction: Confusion about where the config field belonged â€” initial assumption was `[web]` or a top-level section, but it's actually `[server]` via `ServerConfig`; required several grep/read cycles to confirm.
Build ran in background and required polling `TaskOutput` to get results â€” no direct feedback on compilation success.
CUDA crates cause pre-existing build failures on macOS, requiring manual exclusion flags for every clippy/test invocation.
Unsafe code lint blocked the env var override test â€” had to remove it and leave that code path without a direct unit test.
open_items: No docs page exists for the web UI terminal feature â€” the config template comment is the only discoverability mechanism; a hardening/security docs page could reference `MOLTIS_TERMINAL_DISABLED`.
The env var override path (`MOLTIS_TERMINAL_DISABLED=1`) has no unit test due to the unsafe code lint restriction; integration or e2e test coverage would be valuable.

The web UI config editor can still toggle `terminal_enabled` in the TOML and restart the server â€” the env var is the only true hard boundary, but this is by design and noted in the issue discussion.
